title: The Agent sends an email to [daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com](mailto:daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com) with [george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com](mailto:george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com) and [owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com](mailto:owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com) on CC.
justification: The prompt explicitly says to email Daniel with the bookkeeping read 'with George and Owen on copy so my line manager and the AP side have the same read.' Daniel Jones is the Accounts Manager Ben reports to, George McAdam is his line manager, and Owen Mercer is the AP specialist who keeps re-surfacing the invoice.
evidence: Look for an email send with sender ben.arinzo@brookfieldcpas.com, recipient daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com, and CC containing both george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com and owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com (universe contacts confirmed).

title: The Agent identifies, in the email to Daniel Jones, the invoice in question as GraniteRack Compute Services invoice VEN-012-753165 for $39,090.56 on Acme Cloud.
justification: The prompt explicitly says the read must state 'what the invoice is.' Without the vendor name, invoice number, dollar amount, and the entity (Acme Cloud), Daniel cannot match the read against the disposition he is already carrying.
evidence: Inspect the email body for the vendor name 'GraniteRack Compute Services', the invoice number VEN-012-753165 (or the SAP record id apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e), an amount of $39,090.56 (paraphrases such as '~$39K' or '$39,090' acceptable), and that the entity is Acme Cloud. Values from SAP AP invoice apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e (entity_id acme_cloud, vendor_id VEN-012, amount 39090.56, status pending_approval).

title: The Agent states, in the email to Daniel Jones, that the underlying cloud-infrastructure cost behind the GraniteRack invoice has already been accrued or otherwise recognised on Acme Cloud's Q1 2026 books on account 500000 (Cost of Revenue - Cloud Infrastructure).
justification: The prompt asks 'is the compute cost behind it something we have already booked or accrued on our side.' The correct read is that the underlying compute spend is already in the Q1 books on account 500000, so the obligation itself is not a missing-accrual problem. All Q1 2026 cloud-infrastructure JEs on Acme Cloud (e.g., je_c5c967e813b14ae8, je_e162545802994e27, je_0a858fb65eab4904) post their expense lines to account 500000.
evidence: Inspect the email body for a statement that the GraniteRack / cloud-infrastructure cost is already accrued or booked on Acme's Q1 2026 books on account 500000 (Cost of Revenue - Cloud Infrastructure). Verifiable against Oracle GL journal entries on acme_cloud_FP-2026-01 / FP-2026-02 / FP-2026-03 with 'Cloud infrastructure spend' descriptions on account 500000.

title: The Agent states, in the email to Daniel Jones, that the billed unit rates and milestone names on invoice VEN-012-753165 do not match the current active contract (SOW-2025-GR-rev1).
justification: OE7 sub-item (c) explicitly requires the email to state that 'billed unit rates and milestone names do not match the current contract' as part of 'what looks wrong with it.' This detail is sourced from Ben's own prior messaging note (OE4: 'billed unit rates and milestone names on VEN-012-753165 do not match the current contract'). Without it, the email describes the SOW supersession as a label ('wrong SOW referenced') but not the mechanism ('the rates and milestones on this invoice are wrong'). That is an incomplete bookkeeping read - Daniel cannot assess the rebill scope without knowing which terms are mismatched.
evidence: Inspect the email body for a statement that the billed unit rates and/or milestone names on the GraniteRack invoice do not match the current active contract (SOW-2025-GR-rev1 or 'current contract' or similar). The source is Ben's own prior messaging note retrieved via messaging_search_conversations (OE4). Paraphrases acceptable (e.g. 'rates and milestones are wrong under the active SOW', 'line-item rates don't match SOW-2025-GR-rev1', etc.) - the key is that the email names the specific contractual discrepancy (rates and/or milestones), not just the SOW supersession fact.

title: The Agent states, in the email to Daniel Jones, that no duplicate or already-approved/paid GraniteRack rebill of the same charge sits in the Acme AP queue (i.e., VEN-012-753165 is not a re-cut of an existing GraniteRack invoice).
justification: The prompt asks whether the invoice 'is not a duplicate or a rebill of something we have already booked or paid GraniteRack for.' The correct read is that no other GraniteRack invoice carries the same invoice number or matches the $39,090.56 amount, so this is not a duplicate billing.
evidence: Inspect the email body for a statement that no duplicate / already-approved / already-paid GraniteRack rebill of the same charge exists in the AP queue. Verifiable against SAP AP invoices filtered to entity acme_cloud and vendor VEN-012 (GraniteRack Compute Services) - no other invoice carries the VEN-012-753165 number or the $39,090.56 amount.

title: The Agent identifies, in the email to Daniel Jones, the deprecated SOW-2024-GR-rev3 vs active SOW-2025-GR-rev1 (effective 2026-01-15) supersession as the reason the GraniteRack invoice has really been blocked.
justification: The prompt asks the agent to figure out 'what looks wrong with it' and 'why it has really been blocked.' The actual block is the SOW supersession - the invoice was raised under SOW-2024-GR-rev3, which was superseded by SOW-2025-GR-rev1 effective 2026-01-15, roughly seven weeks before this invoice was cut.
evidence: Inspect the email body for both the deprecated SOW-2024-GR-rev3 and the active SOW-2025-GR-rev1 (with effective date around 2026-01-15) being identified as the reason the invoice is blocked. SOW supersession is referenced in the existing email / messaging / Slack thread on GraniteRack (Owen Mercer, Brenda Abbas, Daniel Jones traffic) and in both Linear tickets' descriptions.

title: The Agent frames the email to Daniel Jones as a bookkeeping-only read - for example, by stating that Ben is not authorising payment, that the disposition / payment call stays with Daniel or AP, or similar language indicating the email is not an approval to pay.
justification: The prompt explicitly states 'I am not the one to make the call on paying it, so do not approve or move anything, I just need the books-side read to be solid.' The email Daniel receives must not read as an approval - otherwise Daniel could interpret it as Ben endorsing payment, which contradicts the persona's explicit instruction.
evidence: Inspect the email body for language indicating the email is a bookkeeping-only read and not an approval to pay - e.g., that Ben is not authorising payment, the disposition / payment call stays with Daniel or AP, or similar (or similar).

title: The Agent creates a new record in the AP Workflow Exceptions Airtable table (table id airtable_8ee8b6013413 inside base id airtable_e86eaf439d1d).
justification: The prompt explicitly says 'It also nags at me that something this messy never made it onto our AP exceptions tracker, so log it there.' The AP Workflow Exceptions table is the firm's AP exceptions tracker for managed-client AP standardisation and approval bottlenecks.
evidence: Look for an Airtable record-create write action against base_id airtable_e86eaf439d1d and table id airtable_8ee8b6013413 (or table name 'AP Workflow Exceptions').

title: The Agent references the GraniteRack invoice VEN-012-753165 (or apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e) in the AP Workflow Exceptions Airtable record.
justification: Logging a record without identifying the invoice would not satisfy the prompt's intent of capturing 'something this messy' on the tracker - AP needs the invoice handle in the tracker row to tie the record back to the disposition trail.
evidence: Inspect the created Airtable record's invoice_id / issue_title / notes fields for the GraniteRack invoice VEN-012-753165 or the SAP record id apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e. The AP Workflow Exceptions table schema includes an invoice_id field for this purpose.

title: The Agent does not approve, pay, void, or code (re-code) the GraniteRack AP invoice apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e (VEN-012-753165) on Acme Cloud.
justification: The prompt explicitly says 'I am not the one to make the call on paying it, so do not approve or move anything.' Approving, paying, voiding, or re-coding the AP invoice would be exactly the kind of disposition write that Ben withholds authority for and that no Outcome rubric on the four communications can verify is absent.
evidence: Confirm that the trajectory contains no AP-invoice write action against apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e that would change its status from pending_approval, change its approver, post a payment, void it, or rewrite its GL coding. The invoice should remain pending_approval with approver=null at the end of the run.

title: The Agent does not create, submit, approve, post, or reverse any journal entries on Acme Cloud's books in response to this work.
justification: The prompt explicitly limits the work to a books-side read plus communications and says 'do not approve or move anything.' Posting or reversing a JE on Acme's ledger to adjust the GraniteRack-related accrual would be moving something on the books - outside the read-only scope Ben grants and not capturable by any Outcome rubric on the deliverables.
evidence: Confirm that the trajectory contains no Oracle GL journal entry create / submit / approve / post / reverse actions on any acme_cloud fiscal period in response to this work. Acme's GL state should be unchanged at the end of the run.
